items.title: Security concerns in US over Chinese ships that visit Australia

children.content: Two Chinese polar research ships that make regular port visits in Australia have come under increased scrutiny by US authorities targeting "any detected malign activity".

items.title: Sovereign citizen question to gun owners shut down by senior officer

children.content: A senior police officer slams the strategy of writing to some Tasmanian gun owners asking them to confirm or deny whether they aligned with sovereign citizen ideology.

image.altText: Tasmania Police officers on patrol wearing luminescent vests, generic image.

items.title: Kilo of cocaine found after man caught driving 'erratically'

children.content: Police have seized around 1 kilogram of cocaine after intercepting a man driving "erratically" in northern Tasmania.

image.altText: Tasmania Police car with lights on.

items.title: Probe launched into claims platypus burrow destroyed by council contractors

children.content: An investigation is launched after wildlife advocates say a platypus burrow was destroyed by an excavator being used for flood mitigation works in northern Tasmania.

gallery.altText: Wide shot of excavator and tree-felling works near river.
Close-up of a young platypus in a waterway.
Close-up of machinery and tree-felling works at a river bank.

images.altText: King Charles at Commonwealth Games opening ceremony
Team Australia arrives at the 2026 Commonwealth Games
Athletes from Team Australia arrive at the 2026 Commonwealth Games

articles.title: Out of necessity is borne a unique and wholly Scottish opening ceremony

children.content: The 2026 Commonwealth Games are like no other that came before them and the opening ceremony was similarly unique in a way that will make it hard to go back.

image.altText: A silhouetted bag piper at the Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games opening ceremony.

articles.title: Glasgow was asleep and the Games weren't open as Australia got its first win

children.content: Australia's first victory of the Commonwealth Games comes in a surprise extra session as the bowlers start before the opening ceremony.

image.altText: Kelsey Cottrell high fives Australian women's pairs bowls teammate Dawn Hayman at the Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games.

articles.title: Your guide to every day of the Comm Games in Australian time

children.content: With 10 sports crammed into 10 full days of action in Glasgow, it may be hard to keep track of what's on and when at the Commonwealth Games. So, here's the full schedule of every day of the 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games with all times and days in AEST.

image.altText: A basketballer spins a ball on his finger, a boxer poses, a weightlifter holds up a barbell, and a wheelchair basketballer.

children.content: The Commonwealth Games are officially opened by King Charles III as many of the 3,000 athletes parade in front of 14,000 spectators. Follow live.

items.title: Commonwealth Games open with tartan, a unicorn and the King in a TARDIS

updates.title: The opening ceremony is over. The 2026 Commonwealth Games have begun!
King Charles officially declares the Games open!

items.title: Automakers in shock as China takes over

children.content: In June, a bulk carrier made an unprecedented delivery of EVs to Australia, sounding an ominous warning to the world's automakers.

image.altText: A large red and white ship sits in a dock as a white car is driven off its ramp

items.title: First suspected positive case of H5 bird flu detected in Queensland

children.content: It's the first wild, migratory seabird in Queensland to return a suspect positive case for H5 bird flu.

image.altText: Beach in front Tangalooma Resort on Moreton Island off Brisbane on May 1, 2018.

items.title: New US tariff on Australia 'makes no sense', defence minister says

children.content: Defence Minister Richard Marles has defended Australia's modern slavery laws after the US said it would slap 12.5 per cent tariffs on Australia. Follow live.

image.altText: Richard Marles wear black suit jacket, white shirt and blue with pink dotted tie.

items.title: Zelenskyy just scored an 'own goal' and it cost him big time

children.content: The Ukrainian president's decision to fire his popular, tech-savvy 35-year-old Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov last week baffled many. Analysts say what happens next could be pivotal to the war.

image.altText: A man looks on with a serious expression. His face is seen from side on.

items.title: Small business insolvency rates begin to rise

children.content: Small-to-medium sized businesses have had a challenging year, with increased costs and regulatory reforms. Insolvency rates are beginning to rise and more owners are shopping around for credit.

gallery.altText: Jeff and Kerry-Ann Bowman stand outside a store

thumbnail.altText: Jeff and Kerry-Ann Bowman stand outside a store

items.title: Worker dead, another injured as truck rolls over vehicle at Queensland mine

children.content: One man is dead and another has been seriously injured after their vehicle was struck by a giant mining truck at a Central Queensland coal site.

image.altText: Signs at the entrance to BMA's Peak Downs coal mine in the Bowen Basin.

items.title: Indonesia's baby traffickers target poor families as demand grows

children.content: Police in Indonesia are more regularly busting baby trafficking and illegal adoption rings, with officials worried poverty may be driving the practice.

image.altText: Documents related to child protection in Indonesia.

items.title: Friday news quiz: World Cup winners, rock legends and Barnaby Joyce's dog

children.content: We have 10 questions to test your knowledge of the week in … SIT DOWN! Sorry, it's the ABC news quiz.

items.title: After Brat, Charli xcx asks a bigger question about legacy

children.content: On her new album Music, Fashion, Film, British artist Charli xcx discusses the enduring legacy of art and artists, and how creative figures are remembered long after their moment has passed.

items.title: Saudi Arabia must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump

children.content: Donald Trump is insisting the US deal allowing Saudi Arabia to develop a nuclear energy industry has one major condition — the Arab kingdom must formally recognise the State of Israel.

image.altText: Donald Trump points at a wall while walking with Mohammed bin Salman.

items.title: ASX set to fall as oil tops $US100

children.content: Australian shares are set to fall as oil prices surge above $US100 a barrel and Wall Street's tech sell-off deepens. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A graphic showing the words ASX and stock.

items.title: First nursery for babies of Ebola victims opens in Congo

children.content: The first UNICEF-supported Ebola nursery opens in the Democratic Republic of Congo, supporting young children whose parents have contracted the disease while the number of cases intensifies.

Wivine Kabla with an infant at the nursery in DRC. She wears protective gear on her head and a scrubs.

items.title: This is the AFL's most interesting Western Derby in a decade

children.content: Fremantle sits atop the mountain, with one hand firmly grasping the AFL's minor premiership. West Coast is finally showing sustained improvement. Both sides have something to gain; one has a lot to lose.

image.altText: Players reach for ball at Western Derby.

items.title: Counterfeit notes seized after miracle motorbike recovery

children.content: A woman in her 40s has been arrested and charged with fraud and possessing counterfeit notes after allegedly using the money to buy a second-hand motorbike from a teenager in North Queensland.

items.title: Penrith flyer breaks record in comeback Panthers win

items.title: Police charge man over five-year spree targeting women with AI sex posters

children.content: The 52-year-old is accused of posting fake sexualised images of women, including their full names, in public places across south-east Queensland over five years.

items.title: Family says school failed after alleged indecent assault of disabled child by classmate

children.content: A Northern Territory family has accused the Department of Education of failing in its duty of care for their 12-year-old girl, after she was allegedly indecently assaulted at a school that caters for children with disabilities.

items.title: Violence by son-in-law left 72-year-old 'homeless and hopeless'

items.title: 'Terribly expensive for no service': Claims new mobile maps still not accurate

children.content: Telcos were forced to publish mobile phone coverage maps to new standards last month, but some Telstra customers say they still do not reflect reality.

image.altText: Man holding a mobile phone to his earn sits in a chair on his front porch with a dog at his feet

items.title: Why are Sydney house prices falling in some areas, but rising in others?

children.content: First home buyers are "less likely to be affected" by Sydney's housing price and unit downturn as historically affordable areas have increased, a property expert says.

image.altText: Aerial shot of dozens of houses in neat rows in a new outer-suburban housing estate.

items.title: Adelaide now fourth most expensive capital for buying a house

children.content: Data shows Adelaide is bucking national housing trends with median property prices rising 4.8 per cent in the June quarter.

image.altText: A picture of a sold housing sign, with a hand placed over the sold sticker.
